texto: You can fall out of love accidentally but you can't fall back in consciously, try as you may. It's like waking up out of bed. You look around you, take it all in and, when you're awake, wonder 'how did I get here?' And that's what your heart does sometimes; it keeps its secrets, even from you and throws you back the reins when you're least prepared. By then it's too late.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Phil G Glenn: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “You can fall out of love accidentally but you can't fall back in consciously, try as you may. It'…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, romance-novels, reflexao pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

And that's what your heart does sometimes; it keeps its secrets, even from you and throws you back the reins when you're least prepared. By then it's too late.
